zh.Basing, Hampshire, England: 英國英格蘭哈普爾笛卡爾 巴辛
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Hugh de Poynings於1381年出生在英國英格蘭哈普爾笛卡爾 巴辛時，他的父親Thomas Poynings30歲，母親Lady Joan le Strange43歲。他於1394年10月與Elizabeth de Ferrers在英國哈普爾笛卡爾 巴辛結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1426年12月26日在英國哈普爾霍頓享年45歲。

nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Hugh de Poynings was born in 1381, in Old Basing, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas Poynings, was 30 and his mother, Lady Joan le Strange, was 43. He married Elizabeth de Ferrers in October 1394, in Old Basing, Hampshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died on 26 December 1426, in Chawton, Hampshire, England, at the age of 45.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Hugh de Poynings est né en 1381, à Old Basing, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas Poynings, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Lady Joan le Strange, avait 43 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth de Ferrers en octobre 1394, à Old Basing, Hampshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé le 26 décembre 1426, à Chawton, Hampshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 45 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh de Poynings(이)가 1381년 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 드 바징에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Poynings(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Joan le Strange(은)는 43세였다. 그는 1394년 10월 영국 함프실르 드 바징에서 Elizabeth de Ferrers(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1426년 12월 26일 영국 함프실르 차w턴에서 45세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Hugh de Poynings родился в 1381 г. в местности олд басинг, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Thomas Poynings было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Joan le Strange, 43. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth de Ferrers октябрь 1394 г. в местности олд басинг, хампшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер 26 декабря 1426 г. в местности чавтон, хампшир, Англия в возрасте 45 лет.
